projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

maintenance.notes: Thermador dishwasher. New 2025. Clean filter every 6 months. Warranty: typically 2yr.

observations.summary: The 'dog_run' camera is experiencing technical issues, intermittently displaying an error message stating no frames have been received and showing significant visual glitching. Due to these errors, no activity in the dog run can be observed.

The front yard camera is currently not transmitting video, displaying an error message that indicates no frames have been received. This follows a recent period where the camera had recovered from a similar issue, suggesting a recurring connection problem.

insights.summary: [dog_run] The 'dog_run' camera feed is malfunctioning, showing error messages and heavy visual artifacts.
[front_yard] The front yard camera is failing to stream video, displaying an error message.

The cluster of three unclassified doorbell rings (05:07, 05:08, 05:10) combined with persistent unclassified audio in the backyard (05:12-05:15) suggests repeated, unknown attempts at entry or contact while the house was empty. This pattern of unknown activity warrants immediate investigation, especially given the camera failures.
[front_yard] The front yard camera experienced a brief loss of video transmission.
[dog_run] The dog_run camera is not receiving frames and is displaying a technical error.
[front_yard] The front_yard camera is not providing a video feed, showing an error message instead.

There is a pattern of persistent, unclassified audio events detected in the backyard and front yard (3:55 AM - 4:10 AM) that contradict the visual reports of the area being 'empty' and 'quiet.' This, combined with the front yard camera failure (3:27 AM onwards), suggests that there may be subtle, non-visual activity—such as wildlife or unusual noise—that the system is failing to categorize or record properly.
Dogs appear to be home alone — no family member sighted in 5.4 hours (last seen ~10:50PM). Dogs are active in camera observations.
[front_yard] The front yard camera is not providing a video feed, displaying a 'No frames have been received' error.

The dogs are observed roaming in the front yard at 3:06 AM, which is unusual for this time of night. This activity, coupled with multiple instances of unclassified audio detected in the backyard and dog run (02:46, 03:05, 03:10), suggests the dogs may be interacting with an external stimulus or object, warranting a check on the backyard area.
